title: OSI Mapping Schema
description: Schema for defining bi-directional field mappings between data sources and OSI semantic models.

Mapping.description: A single source-to-target mapping for one table/dataset pair

id.description: Source field(s) that uniquely identify a row in this source. Required when a resolution document targets the same dataset.

filter_forward.description: SQL WHERE condition — only source rows matching this condition are mapped forward to the target. Used for routing one source to multiple targets.

filter_reverse.description: SQL WHERE condition — only target rows matching this condition are mapped reverse to this source. Used for selective reverse propagation.

embedded.description: When true, this mapping extracts a sub-entity from the same source row as a parent mapping. The embedded entity shares the parent's source identity and has no independent existence.

field_mappings.description: Field-level mappings from source to target

ModelRef.description: Reference to a data model. Can be an OSI semantic model (semantic_model + dataset) or an external schema (schema_file).

semantic_model.description: Name of an OSI semantic model

dataset.description: Name of the dataset within the semantic model

model_file.description: Path to the OSI semantic model YAML file

schema_file.description: Path to an external schema file (JSON Schema, OpenAPI, Avro, Protobuf, etc.)

schema_path.description: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) into the schema_file identifying the specific schema to map

FieldMapping.description: Maps a single field between source and target models. Must have either forward_expression (scalar mapping) or nested (array extraction), but not both.

target_field.description: Name of the field in the target model. For nested mappings, this names the source array field.

forward_expression.description: Expression that transforms source into the target field (source → target)

reverse_expression.description: Expression that transforms the target field back into the source (target → source). Omit if the mapping is inherently one-way.

nested.description: Extracts items from an array-typed source field into a separate flat target dataset. Only applicable when the source has array-of-objects fields (e.g., OpenAPI, JSON Schema).

target.description: The flat target dataset for extracted array items

id.description: Field(s) within each array item that uniquely identify it

filter_forward.description: Filter applied to each array item in the forward direction. Only items matching this expression are extracted. Enables routing items to different targets.

filter_reverse.description: Filter applied to target rows when reconstructing the array in the reverse direction.

embedded.description: When true, marks this nested extraction as an embedded sub-entity. The extracted entity shares identity with the array item and has no independent existence in the source.

field_mappings.description: Field-level mappings from array item properties to target fields

priority.description: COALESCE ordering — lower number wins. Only meaningful when the target field's resolution strategy is COALESCE.

timestamp_field.description: Source field driving LAST_MODIFIED resolution. Only meaningful when the target field's resolution strategy is LAST_MODIFIED.

required_reverse.description: When true, the entire row is excluded from reverse output if this field's resolved value is null. Enables insert/delete propagation patterns.

description.description: Human-readable description of the mapping logic

data_loss_warning.description: Warning about potential data loss in the reverse direction

dialect.description: SQL or expression language dialect identifier (e.g. ANSI_SQL, SNOWFLAKE)
